    38

    40	#include "esm_pt_defs.h"

    49	
    50	/****************************************************************************/
    51	/****************  E X T E R N A L    D E F I N I T I O N S  ****************/
    52	/****************************************************************************/
    53	
    54	/****************************************************************************/
    55	/*******************  L O C A L    D E F I N I T I O N S  *******************/
    56	/****************************************************************************/
    57	
    58	/* String representation of ESM procedure transaction status */
    59	const char *esm_pt_state2str( int esmptstate){
    60	static const char *_esm_pt_state_str[ESM_PT_STATE_MAX] = {
    61	  "PROCEDURE TRANSACTION INACTIVE",
    62	  "PROCEDURE TRANSACTION PENDING"
    63	};
    64	  return _esm_pt_state_str[esmptstate];
    65	}
    66	
    67	/* Return the index of the next available entry in the list of procedure
    68	 * transaction data */
    69	static int _esm_pt_get_available_entry(esm_pt_data_t *esm_pt_data);
    70	
    71	/****************************************************************************/
    72	/******************  E X P O R T E D    F U N C T I O N S  ******************/
    73	/****************************************************************************/
    74	
    75	/****************************************************************************
    76	 **                                                                        **
    77	 ** Name:    esm_pt_initialize()                                       **
    78	 **                                                                        **
    79	 ** Description: Initialize ESM procedure transaction data                 **
    80	 **                                                                        **
    81	 ** Inputs:  None                                                      **
    82	 **      Others:    None                                       **
    83	 **                
... [truncated] ...
 (0) otherwise.          **
   117	 **                                                                        **
   118	 ***************************************************************************/
   119	int esm_pt_assign(esm_pt_data_t *esm_pt_data)
   120	{
   121	  LOG_FUNC_IN;
   122	
   123	  /* Search for an available procedure transaction identity */
   124	  int i = _esm_pt_get_available_entry(esm_pt_data);
   125	
   126	  if (i < 0) {
   127	    LOG_FUNC_RETURN (ESM_PT_UNASSIGNED);
   128	  }
   129	
   130	  /* Assign new procedure transaction */
   131	  esm_pt_data->context[i] =
   132	    (esm_pt_context_t *)malloc(sizeof(esm_pt_context_t));
   133	
   134	  if (esm_pt_data->context[i] == NULL) {
   135	    LOG_FUNC_RETURN (ESM_PT_UNASSIGNED);
   136	  }
   137	
   138	  /* Store the index of the next available procedure transaction identity */
   139	  esm_pt_data->index = i + 1;
   140	
   141	  /* An available procedure transaction identity is found */
   142	  esm_pt_data->context[i]->pti = i + ESM_PTI_MIN;
   143	  /* Set the procedure transaction status to INACTIVE */
   144	  esm_pt_data->context[i]->status = ESM_PT_INACTIVE;
   145	  /* Disable the retransmission timer */
   146	  esm_pt_data->context[i]->timer.id = NAS_TIMER_INACTIVE_ID;
   147	  /* Setup retransmission timer parameters */
   148	  esm_pt_data->context[i]->args = NULL;
   149	
   150	  LOG_TRACE(INFO, "ESM-FSM   - Procedure transaction identity %d assigned",
   151	            esm_pt_data->context[i]->pti);
   152	  LOG_FUNC_RETURN (esm_pt_data->context[i]->pti);
   153	}
   154	
   155	/****************************************************************************
   156	 **                                                                        **
   157	 ** Name:    esm_pt_release()                                          **
   158	 **                                                                        **
   159	 ** Description: Release the given procedure transaction identity          **
   160	 **                                                                        **
   161	 ** Inputs:  pti:       The identity of the procedure transaction  **
   162	 **             to release                                 **
   163	 **      Others:    None                                       **
   164	 **                                                                        **
   165	 ** Outputs:     None                                                      **
   166	 **      Return:    RETURNok if the procedure transaction iden-**
   167	 **             tity has been successfully released;       **
   168	 **             RETURNerror otherwise.                     **
   169	 **                                                                        **
   170	 ***************************************************************************/
   171	int esm_pt_release(esm_pt_data_t *esm_pt_data, int pti)
   172	{
   173	  LOG_FUNC_IN;
   174	
   175	  if ( (pti < ESM_PTI_MIN) || (pti > ESM_PTI_MAX) ) {
   176	    LOG_FUNC_RETURN (RETURNerror);
   177	  }
   178	
   179	  /* Get procedure transaction data */
   180	  esm_pt_context_t *ctx = esm_pt_data->context[pti - ESM_PTI_MIN];
   181	
   182	  if ( (ctx == NULL) || (ctx->pti != pti) ) {
   183	    /* Procedure transaction not assigned */
   184	    LOG_FUNC_RETURN (RETURNerror);
   185	  }
   186	
   187	  /* Do not release active procedure transaction */
   188	  if (ctx->status != ESM_PT_INACTIVE) {
   189	    LOG_TRACE(ERROR, "ESM-FSM   - Procedure transaction is not INACTIVE");
   190	    LOG_FUNC_RETURN (RETURNerror);
   191	  }
   192	
   193	  /* Stop the retransmission timer if still running */
   194	  if (ctx->timer.id != NAS_TIMER_INACTIVE_ID) {
   195	    LOG_TRACE(INFO, "ESM-FSM   - Stop retransmission timer %d",
   196	              ctx->timer.id);
   197	    ctx->timer.id = nas_timer_stop(ctx->timer.id);
   198	  }
   199	
   200	  /* Release the retransmisison timer parameters */

reasoning_content:
The user is asking me to fix a CRC computation/checking error in the `esm_pt_start_timer` function located at `openair3/NAS/UE/ESM/esm_pt.c:238`. 

Let me analyze the code I've seen so far:

1. The function `esm_pt_start_timer` is at line 238 in `/repo/openair3/NAS/UE/ESM/esm_pt.c`
2. Looking at the code, I can see potential issues related to CRC computation/checking

Let me examine the function more carefully. The function:
- Takes a message (`OctetString *msg`) as input
- Allocates memory for `ctx->args->msg.value` 
- Copies the message data using `memcpy`
- Sets up a timer with this data

The potential CRC issue could be:
1. The length field might not be set correctly before the CRC is computed
2. The memory allocation might fail but the code doesn't handle it properly
3. There might be an issue with uninitialized data being included in CRC calculation

Let me look at the specific part where the message is being copied:

```c
ctx->args->msg.value = (uint8_t *)malloc(msg->length);
ctx->args->msg.length = 0;

if (ctx->args->msg.value) {
  memcpy(ctx->args->msg.value, msg->value, msg->length);
  ctx->args->msg.length = msg->length;
}
```

I see a potential issue here! The `ctx->args->msg.length` is initialized to 0, then only set to the actual length if the malloc succeeds. However, if the malloc fails (returns NULL), the length remains 0. This could cause CRC computation issues downstream because:
1. If malloc fails, `ctx->args->msg.value` is NULL and `ctx->args->msg.length` is 0


2. But the timer is still started with these potentially invalid parameters
3. Downstream code might compute CRC on a buffer that has length 0 but should have had data

The core problem is a critical memory allocation failure scenario. When `malloc` fails to allocate memory, the code continues execution with an uninitialized or zero-length buffer. This creates a significant risk of incorrect CRC calculations or buffer handling in subsequent processing steps. The function should implement robust error checking to prevent timer initialization with potentially corrupted or incomplete message data.

The current implementation silently fails, allowing a timer to be started with a NULL buffer and zero length. This could lead to unpredictable behavior in the system's error handling and message processing mechanisms. A proper error handling approach would involve immediately returning an error if memory allocation fails, ensuring that no timer is started with invalid parameters.

The code's error handling reveals a critical flaw where memory allocation failure doesn't prevent timer initialization. This means a timer could be started with a NULL buffer, potentially causing downstream issues in message processing and error detection.

I need to verify the exact behavior of `nas_timer_start` when passed a NULL argument, as this could introduce subtle bugs in the system's error management strategy.

The proposed solution involves adding explicit error checking after memory allocation to ensure robust error handling and prevent potential silent failures in the timer mechanism.

The issue is subtle but critical. Looking at the code:

1. The message buffer is allocated: `ctx->args->msg.value = (uint8_t *)malloc(msg->length);`
2. The length is initialized to 0: `ctx->args->msg.length = 0;`
3. Then if the allocation succeeded, the data is copied: `memcpy(ctx->args->msg.value, msg->value, msg->length);`
4. **BUT**: The length is only updated if `ctx->args->msg.value` is non-NULL: `ctx->args->msg.length = msg->length;`

The problem is that if `malloc(msg->length)` returns NULL (allocation failure), then:
- `ctx->args->msg.value` will be NULL
- `ctx->args->msg.length` will remain 0
- But the code will still continue and call `nas_timer_start(sec, cb, ctx->args)` with an inconsistent state where `msg.value` is NULL but the struct is otherwise valid.

More importantly, even when the allocation succeeds, there's a CRC-related issue: **the length field is set to 0 before the memcpy, and only updated after**. If any CRC check is performed on the message buffer before the length is properly set, it will compute CRC on zero-length data, causing false positives or missed errors.

The fix is to:
1. Initialize `ctx->args->msg.length = msg->length;` BEFORE the memcpy, or at least ensure consistency
2. Handle the malloc failure case properly by cleaning up and returning an error

Looking at the `esm_pt_start_timer` function, I can see the CRC computation/checking issue. The problem is on lines 273-274 where the code sets `ctx->args->msg.length = 0;` before checking if the malloc succeeded. This causes a CRC checking error because:

1. The message length is set to 0 initially
2. If `ctx->args->msg.value` is NULL (malloc failed), the length remains 0
3. If `ctx->args->msg.value` is not NULL, the length is set to `msg->length` after the memcpy

However, the issue is that when the timer callback is executed and the message is retransmitted, if the malloc failed (resulting in `ctx->args->msg.value = NULL`), the length would still be 0, which could cause false positives or missed error detection in CRC computations downstream.

The fix is to initialize `ctx->args->msg.length` to `msg->length` first, then set it to 0 only if malloc fails. This way, the length is always consistent with what was intended to be sent.

Let me look at the exact lines to understand the issue better:

```c
ctx->args->msg.value = (uint8_t *)malloc(msg->length);
ctx->args->msg.length = 0;  // This is the problem line

if (ctx->args->msg.value) {
    memcpy(ctx->args->msg.value, msg->value, msg->length);
    ctx->args->msg.length = msg->length;  // Length is only set if malloc succeeded
}
```

The issue is that `ctx->args->msg.length` is set to 0 before checking if malloc succeeded. If malloc fails, the length remains 0, which could cause CRC computation issues downstream when the timer fires and tries to retransmit.

The fix should be:


I'll modify the code to initialize the length after confirming successful memory allocation. This ensures the message length is only set when memory is successfully allocated, preventing potential CRC computation errors. The key is to set the length only after verifying the malloc operation succeeded, maintaining the integrity of the message transmission process.

1	#ifndef _ESM_PT_DEFS_H
     2	#define _ESM_PT_DEFS_H

     4	#include "UTIL/nas_timer.h"
     5	#include "IES/ProcedureTransactionIdentity.h"

     7	/****************************************************************************/
     8	/*********************  G L O B A L    C O N S T A N T S  *******************/
     9	/****************************************************************************/
    10	
    11	/*
    12	 * Minimal and maximal value of a procedure transaction identity:
    13	 * The Procedure Transaction Identity (PTI) identifies bi-directional
    14	 * messages flows
    15	 */
    16	#define ESM_PTI_MIN     (PROCEDURE_TRANSACTION_IDENTITY_FIRST)
    17	#define ESM_PTI_MAX     (PROCEDURE_TRANSACTION_IDENTITY_LAST)
    18	
    19	/****************************************************************************/
    20	/************************  G L O B A L    T Y P E S  ************************/
    21	/****************************************************************************/
    22	
    23	/* Procedure transaction states */
    24	typedef enum {
    25	  ESM_PT_INACTIVE,    /* No procedure transaction exists      */
    26	  ESM_PT_PENDING, /* The UE has initiated a procedure transaction
    27	             * towards the network              */
    28	  ESM_PT_STATE_MAX
    29	} esm_pt_state;
    30	
    31	/* ESM message timer retransmission data */
    32	typedef struct {
    33	  unsigned char pti;      /* Procedure transaction identity   */
    34	  unsigned int count;     /* Retransmission counter       */
    35	  OctetString msg;        /* Encoded ESM message to re-transmit   */
    36	  void *user;             /* user reference - void to avoid cyclic dependency */
    37	} esm_pt_timer_data_t;
    38	
    39	/*
    40	 * --------------------------
    41	 * Procedure transaction data
    42	 * --------------------------
    43	 */
    44	typedef struct {
    45	  unsigned char pti;      /* Procedure transaction identity   */
    46	  esm_pt_state status;    /* Procedure transaction status     */
    47	  struct nas_timer_t timer;   /* Retransmission timer         */
    48	  esm_pt_timer_data_t *args;  /* Retransmission timer parameters data */
    49	} esm_pt_context_t;
    50	
    51	/*
    52	 * ------------------------------
    53	 * List of procedure transactions
    54	 * ------------------------------
    55	 */
    56	typedef struct {
    57	  unsigned char index;    /* Index of the next procedure transaction
    58	                 * identity to be used */
    59	#define ESM_PT_DATA_SIZE (ESM_PTI_MAX - ESM_PTI_MIN + 1)
    60	  esm_pt_context_t *context[ESM_PT_DATA_SIZE + 1];
    61	} esm_pt_data_t;
    62	
    63	#endif
